answer:
**The Falsafa corpus contains discussions of *perfectibilité* (perfectibility), *progrès* (progress), *amélioration* (improvement), and closely related concepts like human, moral, industrial, or societal *perfectionnement*, *développement* (development), or *perfection* primarily in the French-language political, social, and economic theory works of Charles Comte and Charles Dunoyer. These terms appear in the English translations as "perfectibility," "progress," "improvement," and "perfecting," often in philosophical contexts tying liberty, property, industry, race, and civilization to human advancement.**

**No other works in the 37-work corpus engage these concepts substantively. Incidental uses in Indic *smṛti* texts (e.g., *Kātyāyana Smṛti*, *Nārada Smṛti*) refer narrowly to legal/property enhancements (e.g., field improvements), not broader perfectibility or progress.[^1][^2] A single poetic reference in Allama Iqbal's *Bang-E-Dara Part 3* laments educational "progress" without deeper engagement.[^3] No hits for *perfectionnement*, *perfectibilité*, or *développement* in English bodies; poetry, Old English, or other theory works (e.g., Fichte) lack relevant discussion.**

### Key Works and Engagement

1. **Charles Dunoyer, *Nouveau traité d'économie: VOL I* (extensive treatment)**  
   Progress is central: liberty grows from industrial/moral progress; all societal advancement (faculties, industry, morality) extends liberty; races differ in perfectibility/progress.[^4][^5][^6][^7] Improvements in agriculture, manufacture, etc., drive liberty.[^8]

2. **Charles Dunoyer, *Nouveau traité d'économie: VOL II* (extensive treatment)**  
   Focuses on industrial improvements/perfecting (routes, machines, agriculture, arts); societal progress via free industry.[^9][^10]

3. **Charles Comte, *Traité de Législation: VOL I* (substantive)**  
   Human perfectibility as natural; imitation aids it; societal progress preserves natural state.[^11][^12]

4. **Charles Comte, *Traité de Législation: VOL III* (substantive)**  
   Compares species' perfectibility/intellectual development; obstacles to moral improvement.[^13][^14]

5. **Charles Comte, *Traité de Législation: VOL IV* (minor)**  
   References racial perfectibility debates.[^15]

**Charles Comte's *Traité de la propriété* (VOL I/II) frames property as vital for human development/social progress (per metadata), but lacks direct term hits in searchable bodies—likely contextual rather than explicit engagement.[^16] No other volumes/authors qualify.**

[^1]: *Kātyāyana Smṛti*, ch. 64, [p-9829d2](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-katyayana-smrti-1e06d2/64/translation/#p-9829d2).  
[^2]: *Nārada Smṛti*, ch. 1, [p-f6cd82](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-naradasmrti-7650d2/01/translation/#p-f6cd82).  
[^3]: *Bang-E-Dara Part 3*, ch. 22, [p-da36f6](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/22/translation/#p-da36f6).  
[^4]: *Nouveau traité d'économie: VOL I*, ch. 0 (Introduction), [p-a32ab7](https://falsafa.corpus/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-i-6da8ce/00-introduction/translation/#p-a32ab7).  
[^5]: Ibid., [p-b6ad3c](https://falsafa.corpus/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-i-6da8ce/00-introduction/translation/#p-b6ad3c).  
[^6]: Ibid., ch. 1, [p-b8546d](https://falsafa.corpus/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-i-6da8ce/01-ce-que-lauteur-entend-par-le-mot-liberte/translation/#p-b8546d).  
[^7]: Ibid., ch. 2, [p-4b4f8f](https://falsafa.corpus/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-i-6da8ce/02-i-influence-de-la-race-sur-la-liberte/translation/#p-4b4f8f).  
[^8]: Ibid., ch. 0, [p-2f445f](https://falsafa.corpus/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-i-6da8ce/00-introduction/translation/#p-2f445f).  
[^9]: *VOL II*, ch. 14, [p-651f43](https://falsafa.corpus/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-ii-d6ae03/14/translation/#p-651f43).  
[^10]: Ibid., ch. 17, [p-9a36a0](https://falsafa.corpus/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-ii-d6ae03/17/translation/#p-9a36a0).  
[^11]: *Traité de Législation: VOL I*, ch. 5, [p-60a286](https://falsafa.corpus/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-i-7e62a2/05-des-lois-auxquelles-les-hommes-sont-assujettis-par/translation/#p-60a286).  
[^12]: Ibid., ch. 11, [p-1882a8](https://falsafa.corpus/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-i-7e62a2/11-chapitre-premier/translation/#p-1882a8).  
[^13]: *VOL III*, ch. 47, [p-d5d44e](https://falsafa.corpus/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-iii-ca589a/47-parallele-entre-les-diverses-especes-d-hommes-de-l/translation/#p-d5d44e).  
[^14]: Ibid., [p-696635](https://falsafa.corpus/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-iii-ca589a/47-parallele-entre-les-diverses-especes-d-hommes-de-l/translation/#p-696635).  
[^15]: *VOL IV*, ch. 23, [p-24dc2b](https://falsafa.corpus/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-iv-bca040/23-de-l-inegalite-des-fortunes-produite-par-l-esclava/translation/#p-24dc2b).  
[^16]: *Traité de la propriété: VOL I* metadata/work description (https://falsafa.corpus/works/charles-comte-traite-de-la-propriete-vol-i-2c7a99/).
